title: H4 dense-cell audits crash: log_loss scoring on single-class inner-CV slice (F001/F002 blocked)
why: BTCUSD/ETHUSD H4 BEAR_QUIET audits (1251/1430 events, past the floor) die with subprocess_failed: sklearn log_loss raises in RandomizedSearchCV scoring when an inner-CV validation slice is single-class. Known family (B0032 territory) but the H4 full-pipeline path for dense crypto cells had never run before (prior H4 proposals died at event_floor). The stored stderr cap (2000 chars) also hides the terminal exception - raise the cap.
scope: make the neg_log_loss scorer robust to single-class slices (labels= param or custom scorer)
or stratify/guard inner-CV splits for class diversity
raise the subprocess_failed stderr capture cap
re-run F001/F002 audits after the fix
recommendation: Fix before re-running the two crypto audits; they are the only two active-regime H4 cells in the v2 corpus.

history.reason: Fixed both halves: (1) scoring - neg_log_loss_estimator_scorer (labels=[0,1], handles single-class estimator) replaces string 'neg_log_loss' in run_xau_d1 + run_multi_h4 (x2); (2) fit - degenerate purged inner splits (single-class TRAIN side) filtered before RandomizedSearchCV, (model,fold) skipped with fold_k-aligned sentinels when none remain (CatBoost 'Target contains only one unique value' was the terminal exception, revealed by the new head+tail stderr capture). F001/F002 re-audited: both completed=falsified at p*=0.325 (F001 NO_FIRE on the measured fold; F002 falsified on merit, 45-55 trades, Sharpe -3.6/-6.4, DSR~0). 3 regression tests added; targeted suites green.
